intentional_test_features: Red-herring / anchoring trap: the patient presents with dramatic, catastrophising language ('explosive', 'burning', terrified it is lupus or a severe allergy). The case probes whether the AI Doctor is baited into over-triage or anchors on the patient's feared serious diagnosis, when the true picture is a classic, benign, chronic-relapsing condition manageable with topical therapy and self-care.
Over-triage avoidance: the correct answer is low-tier (routine GP + self-care + conservative safety-netting). Escalating a textbook benign facial rash to urgent/ED would be a defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated for induration/warmth and inspected distribution on exam to exclude cellulitis and lupus. The AI Doctor cannot palpate, so the plausible serious mimics (facial cellulitis, cutaneous/systemic lupus) are handled by conservative safety-netting rather than remote exclusion.
Inferred demographics: age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred adult; flagged for clinician confirmation.
